comment_on_allowFlags: Native tools (Bash, Read, Write, Edit) govern these at the tool layer; MCP servers are denied by default and must be explicitly approved
fallbackPolicy: Prefer approved MCP servers for personalized/current context. If unavailable, fall back to repository-native CLI or local files and report the fallback in evidence.

rag-index.use_when: repo-scoped semantic search over memory, plans, handoffs, code, and commits — 'what did we decide about X', 'where did we hit this before'
rag-index.do_not_use_when: exact symbol/definition lookup (use serena or grep instead); current library/API docs (use context7)
rag-index.fallback: rg/grep over local memory files and repo docs

github.use_when: PR/issue/repo metadata operations against the GitHub API (create/search/review PRs and issues, check status)
github.do_not_use_when: local-only git operations (branch, commit, diff, log) — use the native git CLI via Bash, no API round-trip needed

filesystem.use_when: cross-directory file operations spanning multiple allowed roots in one call (Desenvolvimento, .claude, .codex)
filesystem.do_not_use_when: single-file read/write/edit in the current repo — use the native Read/Write/Edit/Glob tools, they're faster and don't need an MCP round-trip

git.use_when: not currently observed in active use this session — the native git CLI via Bash covers essentially all git operations already
git.do_not_use_when: prefer native git CLI via Bash for all git operations unless a specific gap is identified

fetch.use_when: fetching a single, publicly-accessible URL provided by the user or already-known to be unauthenticated
fetch.do_not_use_when: auth-gated or JS-rendered SPA pages (confirmed to fail — returns only the app shell, not content); use browser automation (claude-in-chrome) via the user's logged-in session instead
fetch.fallback: WebSearch for open-web info; ask the user to paste content for auth-gated pages

context7.use_when: current documentation for a library, framework, SDK, API, or CLI tool — even well-known ones, since training data may be stale
context7.do_not_use_when: general web research/news (use WebSearch); internal repo code (use serena/grep); refactoring or business-logic debugging

memory.use_when: cross-project decisions/memory recall (the knowledge-brain vault) — 'what did we decide', preferred over rag-index specifically for decision history
memory.do_not_use_when: repo-scoped code/commit search (use rag-index instead — the vault is decisions-only, no code or commit history)
memory.fallback: rag-index, or grep memory files directly

firecrawl.use_when: crawling or scraping an entire site or a multi-page doc set
firecrawl.do_not_use_when: a single URL fetch — use fetch/WebFetch instead, firecrawl is heavier and not needed for one page

meta.note: Extends the approvedServers allowlist above with a per-server intent-to-tool routing contract, per the Reinforce-Support-Layers plan's Phase 3 proposal (scoped down: no separate harness-doctor.sh or routing-eval fixtures yet — see .claude/plans/2026-07-01-skill-consolidation-reinforcement.md Phase 3 for the full classification). expected_callers lists are best-effort based on skills observed referencing each server this session, not an exhaustive audit.
